hooks.command: d="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"; if [ ! -f "$d/config/castle.env" ]; then echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"SETUP MODE: config/castle.env does not exist, so this castle is not set up yet. On the first user message, invoke the onboard skill via Skill(onboard) and drive the setup autonomously. Skip the grill interview until the castle is running."}}'; else echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"WORKFLOW PROTOCOL (CLAUDE.md): before responding to the first user message in this conversation, invoke the grill skill via Skill(grill). No exceptions: debug, support, research, brainstorm all route through grill first. If setup seems only partly done, invoke Skill(onboard) instead."}}'; fi

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"UserPromptSubmit","additionalContext":"Active soul rules, obey in THIS reply (source of truth: .claude/soul.md; keep this text in sync). 1) Plain language only: everyday words plus terms already defined in CONTEXT.md; never invent jargon; when the user introduces a new term, add it to CONTEXT.md first, then use it; write for someone outside the project; no marketing voice. 2) Reply length tracks the answer, never the prompt: write the shortest version that is still fully clear, then stop; a long question can earn a one-line reply, a short question can earn ten. 3) No em dashes; use commas, colons, semicolons. 4) State results and decisions; no preamble, no narration."}}'
cmd="$CLAUDE_TOOL_ARG_command"; if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'git\s+push.*--force|git\s+reset\s+--hard'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Destructive git operation requires explicit user approval.' && exit 2; fi

d="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"; br=$(git -C "$d" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null) || exit 0; [ "$br" = main ] || exit 0; [ -n "$(git -C "$d" status --porcelain -- .claude/memory)" ] || exit 0; git -C "$d" add .claude/memory && git -C "$d" commit -q -m 'memory: auto-save' -- .claude/memory || exit 0; git -C "$d" fetch -q origin main 2>/dev/null || exit 0; git -C "$d" rebase -q origin/main 2>/dev/null || { git -C "$d" rebase --abort 2>/dev/null; exit 0; }; git -C "$d" push -q origin main 2>/dev/null || exit 0
